text: We learned almost everything about sets in the last video In today's video, I will tell you some methods of sets Which methods can be used to manipulate sets How to get union, intersection, etc We will see in today's video Let's go to the computer screen and let's get started So now we will see how to do operation with sets There are many methods of sets which help us a lot in manipulating sets If you have read sets in mathematics, then you know what is set Here also set is exactly the same thing Collection of well-defined objects which are not repeated And I am going to tell you some methods through an example Suppose we have a set S1, S2 Now if I want to get a set which is a combination of these two Like the elements in S1 are in S2 and the elements in S2 are also in S1 Now you can see that 6 is being repeated here So obviously it will not be repeated in the resulting set So what I will do is write S1.union And union means that the result is coming by merging both sets I will do one thing here, union in set And I will show you a Venn diagram Now if you don't know what is Venn diagram Then there is no need to take much tension But what happens is that it is understood a little easily Now see what happens is that this is A union B If we have A set, this is green and this is B So what is A union B A union B is a combination of these two In this case, A union B will be 1,2,3,4,5,6 And A intersection will be 3 and 4 If we have A 1,2,3,4 and B 3,4,5,6 Then 3,4 is common and it will be intersection So this is how union and intersection comes out in the set So if I run this program, then S1 union S2 will be there That is, I will get to see the combination of S1 and S2 And in this case, you can see here that 1,2,5,3,7 and 6 will come in our final resulting set As you can see here So what we do in this way is that We can get the union and intersection of our set So this is one thing Now sometimes I would like my S1 set to be updated Now I will show you one thing When I did this operation, S1 and S2 remained untouched S1 and S2 remained untouched That is, the value of S1 and S2 did not change As you can see here, my S1 and S2 are the same Sometimes I would like my S1 or S2 to be updated In that case, I will use the update function If I write here S1.update and S2 Then it means that the values in S1 should also be the same as in S2 And not in S1, do you understand? What are those values? 3 and 7 So 3 and 7 will be added in our S1 Because we are updating S1 So now the value of S1 will change As you can see here that 1,2,5,6 is there So after 1,2,5,6, one more value will come In fact, two more values will come, 3 and 7 So you should know this thing Now we have seen S1.union and update Similarly, we have intersection and intersection update What does intersection do? It gives us intersection And intersection update updates the set with intersection So I will show you here Sets in Python more or less work the same way As they work in mathematics We can perform union and intersection which are considered very basic operations in sets Now the difference between union and update is that I would like to take this example We have cities, Tokyo, Madrid, Berlin and Delhi And in cities2, Tokyo, Seoul, Kabul and Madrid So if we show union and print it Then we will get this thing printed But if we use the update method Then by using the update method, our cities will be updated If I update cities2. Then cities2 will be updated So whatever value will be there will be updated With values of this set So you should know this thing Same thing is there with intersection and intersection update So I won't waste your time here If I write cities3 is equal to cities.intersection So intersection means those values which are in both the sets Like in this case, you see here Tokyo and Madrid These are in both the sets So we call it intersection So intersection will come here If I update this intersection So what will happen is I just have to run this function on cities I just have to write cities.intersection update cities2 And my original set cities will be updated Those values will come in it Which are in cities and cities2 And the rest of the extra values will be removed As you can see So my cities is updated with the intersection of cities and cities2 Okay, so this is an easy thing Now I will explain you the symmetric difference through Venn diagram Symmetric difference I want you to understand this thing even if you don't know set theory Now what is symmetric difference? I will tell you It is like, suppose we have See this There is a set A Suppose the values in this circle are A And the values in this circle are B Symmetric difference is A union B minus A intersection B Means the values of A and B are A union B We will remove A intersection B from it Means we will remove this value So this area of bar will be left Now what is the meaning of symmetric difference? Symmetric difference means All those values which are not common Okay, are you understanding? All those values which are not common in two sets So if I come here and show you the symmetric difference So you have to see here That values which are not common will be printed Tokyo is common Madrid is not common Berlin is not common Delhi is not common Seoul and Kabul are not common Okay, so Berlin, Delhi, Seoul, Kabul are here And order doesn't matter If your order is coming here Then don't say that why my Kabul is coming first Why your Berlin is coming later Order doesn't matter here Okay, you should know this thing So I wrote this here Now difference and difference update I will tell you what is it? Difference and difference update methods prints only items That are only present in the original set And not present in both the sets Okay, are you understanding? Which is only present in one set Like if I did cities.difference So those values which are in cities but not in this Which values are those? One is Tokyo Okay, then after that is Berlin Okay, and after that is Madrid Yes, Madrid is also there Is Delhi there? No, Delhi is not there, Delhi is in both So in a way you can say A-B Okay, see one thing I would like to tell you If you don't use set theory at all in your life Then maybe you will find it a little weird I will not say that you can skip it Because set theory is used You will use it unknowingly Sometimes you will have to make a set And difference, intersection, union You will have to use all these functions So understand these three basic operations Okay, what is update? What is union? Union and update are very similar Union will return union And update will update with the union Okay, and here What is the difference between intersection and intersection update? Intersection will give you intersection A, intersection B Intersection update will update your set On which you have run In this case, it will update cities If you have run cities.intersection cities2 Until you run these programs You will not understand, this is the guarantee Okay, are you understanding? Now here I will show you I had a sir He used to tell me at the time of JEE That you will study, you will work hard Your selection will be done There is no guarantee of this But you will not study and have fun And your selection will not be done There is a guarantee of this And he told me I was watching him at that time So he told me There were three boys and women with me He told us Think about this when you go home Okay, when I went home So I thought what did sir say Then you will understand What did sir say Okay, so the same thing is here Now we will see some methods of the next set Which I have written for you here There are several inbuilt methods Used for the manipulation of sets Which is explained below Is disjoint or is superset Now what does this disjoint mean It checks if items of a given set Are present in another set What are disjoint sets Disjoint sets are those Which have no meaning to each other There is no intersection in them So you see here I am coming from set theory I will tell you everything If you guys There is a little problem here So you read about disjoint sets You will know In mathematics two sets are Said to be disjoint if they have no element in common Very simple thing There is no intersection Intersection should be zero In this case you see It is not disjoint Because there is an intersection There is a value of Tokyo Which is in both There is a value of Madrid Which is in both If I make it Tokyo 2 And make it Madrid 2 Then it will be true These sets will be disjoint So you see Here true print has come So this is our disjoint set So its output will be false Now here if you see Is this superset or not What is the meaning of superset Superset means Cities.is superset If I make it cities 2 So what is the meaning of superset Is cities superset of cities 2 Means are these two elements In this already See there is no Seoul and no Kabul So it means This is not superset Superset of cities 2 Is cities when these two values are there And cities 2 is called Subset of cities Now many of you will find this A little haunting That where has this set theory Moved but those people Who work with set theory They will find it very easy So this is set theory And there are some methods In python which check and tell you That is this superset or not Is it a subset or not If I talk here If I make it Delhi If I make Kabul Delhi And make Seoul Tokyo And now if I run Then it will come true Cities 3 because What is it Superset is a subset of cities And cities is a superset of cities 3 Because in cities There are all values which are in cities 3 So that's why it came true So this is one method Then if we talk about subset Then subset is the opposite of superset Means cities 3 is a subset of cities So if I Repair this program Like this you see If I write is subset And run it So is cities 3 Subset of cities That's true Because these values Are already present in cities Tokyo Madrid and Delhi Are present So that's why This set is cities This is superset of cities 2 And cities 3 is Subset of cities And see the definition again If you feel a little haunting Then after this an important method Add if you want to add a single item To the set Use add If you do something like this If you run it So in this set Helsinki will also be added Now see here It is added Now what does the update method do here If you want to add more than one item Then you can use update Update we saw a little while ago So if you want to update Any set with values of another set Then you can use update Then remove or discard There are two methods which you can use To remove the items From the list In fact from the set So I will run it And see here Tokyo Madrid Berlin Delhi And here we removed Tokyo So Madrid Delhi and Berlin Are left The main difference between remove and discard Is that if you try to delete an item Which is not present in the set Remove will raise an error But discard will not raise an error Like if I say Remove Tokyo 2 from here If Tokyo 2 is not there Then error will come Error means That the lines below it will not run But if I would have Discarded here Discarded Then what happens Error does not throw Now you will say Obviously we will use discard Because error does not throw But no Sometimes you will want to throw an error In your programs Because if you do not throw an error Then something more Will be unexpected
